title: Origami fish, folding a simple fish from a square sheet
subtitle: A beginner folding sequence that produces a flat fish with a recognisable tail and body
excerpt: This origami fish uses the fish base, one of the four fundamental origami bases, and is completed in twelve steps. The result is a flat, stylised fish with a pointed head, a fanned tail, and two distinct body planes. It is a good introduction to petal folds and the fish base fold sequence.
sourceNotes: Traditional Japanese origami. The fish base is one of the classical origami bases documented in Japanese paper-folding manuals from the Edo period. The construction described here follows the standard fish base sequence as documented in Western origami literature from the early 20th century.

glossaryTerms.definition: One of the four fundamental origami bases. Produced by folding two adjacent corners of a square to the centre line, then applying petal folds to the resulting flaps. The fish base has two elongated points and two short flaps.
An origami manoeuvre in which a triangular flap is opened and squash-folded into a symmetrical petal shape. The petal fold creates the fish's tail in this model.

content.text: This fish uses the
fish base

. The base itself is worth learning as a separate skill since it appears in several other models. A mini bone folder pressed along each fold before moving to the next step keeps the layers flat and makes the
petal fold
more precise, though the model can be made entirely by hand.
Step 1, Crease the diagonals

Start with a square, coloured side up if applicable. Fold corner to corner along both diagonals, creasing firmly, then unfold. You now have two diagonal crease lines crossing the centre of the square.
Step 2, Fold two edges to the centre
Turn the square so one corner points away from you. Fold the left and right edges inward to meet the vertical centre crease, producing a narrow kite shape. Crease firmly.
Step 3, Repeat on the other axis

Unfold the two flaps. Rotate the paper 90 degrees and fold the left and right edges to the centre again along the other axis. You have now established the two sets of kite folds needed for the fish base.
Step 4, Fold the nose corner down
With both sets of edge folds in place and oriented so the paper is a kite, fold the top point down to meet the bottom point. Crease firmly across the middle.
Step 5, Petal fold the upper flap
Lift the top layer of the lower flap and open it upward, squash-folding it flat so that the fold edges align with the centre vertical line. This is the
petal fold
. The flap becomes a symmetrical diamond shape pointing upward.
Step 6, Form the tail

Fold the diamond flap back down and spread the two halves apart to form the tail fins. Adjust the angle of each fin to match, then flatten and crease the base of the tail. Turn the model over, the smooth side is the fish's body. Bring the two side points forward slightly to give the body a three-dimensional profile if desired, or leave the model flat.
Using the fish base
The
fish base

is the starting point for several other origami models including the butterfly and the crane variant. Practising the base fold several times from the same size square builds the muscle memory needed for more complex models that begin with it.
